summary: Drew DeVault's January 2021 status update covers progress across several projects. The headline item is godocs.io, a community fork of Google's godoc.org that removes Google dependencies and the CLA requirement. SourceHut gained a dark theme and began work on API 2.0 for todo.sr.ht. Several smaller projects received community contributions. The post closes with a teaser showing working string handling code in what would become the Hare programming language.
key_points: godocs.io launched as a community-run fork of godoc.org, removing Google's CLA and GCP dependencies in favor of PostgreSQL
SourceHut added automatic dark theme support and began implementing GraphQL-based API 2.0 for todo.sr.ht and hg.sr.ht
Multiple small projects (mkproof, gmnisrv, gmni, dowork) received community contributions and bugfixes
A code sample reveals early progress on string handling in the Hare programming language, showing UTF-8 storage, pointer casting, and memory layout verification
money_quotes: Our fork has already attracted interest from many contributors who wanted to work on godoc.org, but found the Google CLA distasteful.

We've been hard at work excising lots of Google crap, rewriting the indexer to use PostgreSQL instead of GCP, and making the little JavaScript bits more optional & more conservative in their implementation.
Beyond this, we do not plan on making any large-scale changes: we just want godoc.org to keep being a thing.
I have actually been working on this a lot this month. Progress is good.
key_insight: Community-driven forks can preserve valuable internet infrastructure when corporate stewardship falters, as demonstrated by godocs.io replacing Google's abandoned godoc.org.
